greet.text: Rhea changes your dressing with hands that are gentler than her face. "Don't thank me. I voted to leave you in the alley." She ties off the bandage with a small, precise tug. "Jackie hauled you up here herself. Beauvoir's been waiting two days for you to stop drooling. Try to be worth it."

options.text: "Why would you leave me in an alley?"

deck.text: "Two-a-Day's got it, up top, sweating over what you did to his consignment ice." A grim little smile. "Man borrowed god-money to rent that slab and lent it to a wilson for a field test. You want the deck, take it up with him. Bring a mop for the flop sweat."

options.text: "Why would you leave me in an alley?"

why.text: "Because whatever looked at you in that ice looked *back up the wire*, little man. People who get noticed like that are lightning rods, and I live in this building." She packs her kit. "But Jackie says the Virgin covered you with her own hands, and Jackie would know. So. Altar room. Walk soft."

bye.text: "Go on," she says, already stripping the table, done with you in the way of medics everywhere: alive is alive, next.
